title: A Hymn to God the Father by Ben Jonson — Love Poetry Lines
content:
Hear me, O God!
A broken heart
Is my best part.
Use still thy rod,
That I may prove
Therein thy Love.

If thou hadst not
meaning: "A Hymn to God the Father" by Ben Jonson is a love and deep poem consisting of 37 lines. The poet explores themes of love and deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Hear me, O God! A broken heart...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Ben Jonson's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
